critical_issues.title: Excessive Kitchen Preparation Time
critical_issues.root_cause: Average kitchen time of 31.6 minutes significantly exceeds industry standard of 15-20 minutes. Peak times show kitchen times exceeding 45 minutes, indicating capacity constraints, workflow inefficiencies, or understaffing during busy periods.
critical_issues.impact: Customer dissatisfaction, reduced table turnover, potential order cancellations
critical_issues.recommendation: Analyze kitchen workflow during peak hours (12-2 PM, 7-10 PM), implement prep station optimization, consider adding kitchen staff during rush periods, and introduce kitchen display system for better order management.

critical_issues.title: High Order Cancellation Rate
critical_issues.root_cause: 26 cancelled orders out of 343 total orders (7.6% cancellation rate) is significantly above industry benchmark of 2-3%. Pattern shows cancellations occurring primarily for dine-in orders with no completion time recorded.
critical_issues.recommendation: Implement order confirmation protocol within 2 minutes, train staff on managing customer expectations, investigate reasons for cancellations through customer feedback, and consider deposit system for high-value orders.

critical_issues.title: Inconsistent Service Speed Across Order Types
critical_issues.root_cause: Takeaway orders averaging 31.2 minutes vs dine-in at 31.9 minutes shows minimal differentiation. Some takeaway orders exceed 45 minutes, suggesting kitchen prioritization issues and lack of separate prep workflows.
critical_issues.impact: Lost takeaway business due to slow service, customer complaints
critical_issues.recommendation: Create dedicated takeaway prep station, implement priority queue system for takeaway orders (target 20 minutes), use separate ticket system, and train staff on parallel order processing.

critical_issues.title: Revenue Concentration Risk
critical_issues.root_cause: Several extremely high-value orders (₹140,400, ₹139,000, ₹111,700, ₹116,600) indicate dependency on large orders. Loss of such customers would significantly impact revenue. Average order value of ₹40,872 shows high variability.
critical_issues.impact: Revenue volatility and business sustainability risk
critical_issues.recommendation: Develop loyalty program for high-value customers, create mid-tier menu packages to increase average order value consistency, implement customer relationship management system, and diversify revenue streams.

critical_issues.title: Peak Hour Kitchen Bottlenecks
critical_issues.root_cause: Kitchen times during lunch (12-2 PM) and dinner (7-10 PM) peaks frequently exceed 40-45 minutes, with some orders taking 47+ minutes. This indicates capacity constraints during high-demand periods.
critical_issues.impact: Reduced table turnover rate, customer walkouts, negative reviews
critical_issues.recommendation: Implement dynamic staffing model with additional kitchen staff during peaks, introduce simplified express menu for faster preparation, optimize ingredient prep work during off-peak hours, and consider kitchen equipment upgrade.

critical_issues.title: Low Table Turnover Efficiency
critical_issues.root_cause: Combined long kitchen times (31.6 min average) with dine-in service creates extended table occupancy. Some tables show only 2-3 seatings per meal period when 4-5 should be achievable.
critical_issues.recommendation: Reduce kitchen time to under 25 minutes, implement table time limits during peak hours, train servers on polite table management, optimize order-taking process, and introduce online ordering for faster service.

critical_issues.title: Cancelled High-Value Orders
critical_issues.root_cause: Multiple high-value cancelled orders including ₹99,200, ₹72,500, ₹47,100, ₹37,100, and ₹36,400 suggest issues with large party handling, kitchen capacity communication, or order accuracy confirmation.
critical_issues.recommendation: Implement pre-confirmation call for orders above ₹50,000, provide realistic time estimates, assign dedicated staff for high-value orders, and create large order management protocol.

critical_issues.title: Evening Service Performance Decline
critical_issues.root_cause: Evening orders (7-10 PM) show consistently longer kitchen times (35-48 minutes) compared to lunch service, suggesting staff fatigue, ingredient depletion, or equipment issues during extended service hours.
critical_issues.impact: Customer complaints, reduced evening revenue potential
critical_issues.recommendation: Schedule shift rotation to ensure fresh kitchen staff for dinner service, implement mid-day ingredient restocking protocol, conduct equipment maintenance between lunch and dinner, and monitor staff performance metrics by shift.
